Off Calendar Jun-11-2014 Text Ruling

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-27-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Goodroe, Esq., 510.969.2369, for plaintiff Susan Koopman.
Jeremy Cleveland, Esq., 925.939.5330, for defendant Ramos Oil Company, Inc.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT RAMOS OIL COMPANY, INC DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. The existence of a duty is usually determined by a summary judgment motion because the analysis is somewhat factual. For instance, it is a factual issue whether there have been prior similar incidents. At any rate, plaintiff pleads sufficient facts to create a duty under Rowland v. Christian, (1968) 69 Cal.2d 108, 113. The injury was generally foreseeable, defendant owns the hoses, and imposing a duty to warn or instruct on defendant is not burdensome. Plaintiff correctly notes that the doctrine of assumption of the risk does not apply to admiralty cases. See De Sole v. United States (1991 4th Cir.) 947 F.2d 1169, 1174. Lastly, the worker's compensation argument is untimely because it was raised in the reply brief and it is also frivolous. Worker's compensation exclusivity does not apply because plaintiff and defendant do not have an employment relationship. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Joyce Finley #8620.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-04-2014 Granted Per Written Order

Jeremy G. Zeitlin, Esq. of Gibson Robb & Lindh LLP, Tel. 415.348.6000, for defendant Red & White Fleet.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court vacates its tentative ruling and grants PLAINTIFF SUSAN KOOPMAN'S Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
